from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    parts = relationship('BicyclePart', backref='bicycle')
    accessories = relationship('Accessory', backref='bicycle')

class BicyclePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bicycle_shop.db')
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bicycle
bicycle = Bicycle(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', price=500)
session.add(bicycle)
session.commit()

# Add a new bicycle part
part = BicyclePart(name='Tire', price=50, bicycle=bicycle)
session.add(part)
session.commit()

# Add a new accessory
accessory = Accessory(name='Helmet', price=30, bicycle=bicycle)
session.add(accessory)
session.commit()

# Update a bicycle
bicycle.price = 550
session.commit()

# Delete a bicycle part
session.delete(part)
session.commit()

# Filter and select bicycles
all_bicycles = session.query(Bicycle).all()
mountain_bikes = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(model='Mountain Bike').all()

# Search for a bicycle by model
bike = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(model='Mountain Bike').first()

session.close()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

# Create engine and Session
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bicycle_shop.db')
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)

# Declarative base
Base = declarative_base()

# Define models
class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)

    parts = relationship('Part', backref='bicycle')
    accessories = relationship('Accessory', backref='bicycle')

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

# Create tables
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Open connection and perform operations
session = Session()

# Add new bicycle
new_bicycle = Bicycle(name='Mountain Bike', description='High-performance mountain bike', price=500)
session.add(new_bicycle)
session.commit()

# Add new part
new_part = Part(name='Wheel', description='Front wheel', price=50, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_part)
session.commit()

# Add new accessory
new_accessory = Accessory(name='Helmet', description='Safety helmet', price=30, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_accessory)
session.commit()

# Update bicycle
bicycle = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(id=new_bicycle.id).first()
bicycle.price = 550
session.commit()

# Delete part
part = session.query(Part).filter_by(id=new_part.id).first()
session.delete(part)
session.commit()

# Filtered selection
filtered_bicycles = session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.price > 400).all()

# Search
searched_bicycles = session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.name.like('%Bike%')).all()

# Close connection
session.close()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

# Create a connection to the database
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bicycle_shop.db')
Base = declarative_base()

# Define the tables
class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)

import sqlalchemy as sa
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class OnlineShop(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'online_shops'

    id = sa.Column(sa.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = sa.Column(sa.String(100), nullable=False)
    description = sa.Column(sa.Text)
    url = sa.Column(sa.String(200), nullable=False)
    created_at = sa.Column(sa.DateTime, default=sa.func.now())
    updated_at = sa.Column(sa.DateTime, onupdate=sa.func.now())

    products = sa.orm.relationship('Product', backref='online_shop')

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = sa.Column(sa.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = sa.Column(sa.String(100), nullable=False)
    description = sa.Column(sa.Text)
    price = sa.Column(sa.Numeric(10,2), nullable=False)
    stock = sa.Column(sa.Integer, nullable=False)
    created_at = sa.Column(sa.DateTime, default=sa.func.now())
    updated_at = sa.Column(sa.DateTime, onupdate=sa.func.now())

    online_shop_id = sa.Column(sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey('online_shops.id'), nullable=False)

engine = sa.create_engine('sqlite:///online_shop.db')

session = Session()

session.add(new_shop)
session.commit()

session.add(new_product)
session.commit()

import psycopg2
from datetime import datetime

def create_database_schema():
    try:
        conn = psycopg2.connect(
            dbname="your_database_name",
            user="your_username",
            password="your_password",
            host="your_host_address"
        )
        cur = conn.cursor()

        # Create the client table
        cur.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE clients (
                client_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
                client_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
                client_address VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
                contact_person VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
                contact_phone VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
                contact_email VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            )
        """)

        # Create the invoices table
        cur.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE invoices (
                invoice_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
                client_id INTEGER REFERENCES clients(client_id),
                invoice_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
                invoice_date DATE NOT NULL,
                due_date DATE NOT NULL,
                total_amount DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
                paid_amount DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0.00,
                status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            )
        """)

        # Create the payments table
        cur.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE payments (
                payment_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
                invoice_id INTEGER REFERENCES invoices(invoice_id),
                payment_date DATE NOT NULL,
                payment_amount DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
                payment_method VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
                payment_note TEXT,
                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            )
        """)

        conn.commit()
        cur.close()
        conn.close()

        created_at TEXT NOT NULL
    )""")

        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,

    conn = sqlite3.connect(database_name)
    c = conn.cursor()
    password_hash = hashlib.sha256(password.encode()).hexdigest()
    created_at = datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    try:
        c.execute("INSERT INTO users (username, password_hash, email, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)", (username, password_hash, email, created_at))
        conn.commit()
        conn.close()
        return True
    except sqlite3.IntegrityError:
        conn.close()
        return False

    conn = sqlite3.connect(database_name)
    c = conn.cursor()
    password_hash = hashlib.sha256(password.encode()).hexdigest()
    c.execute("SELECT id FROM users WHERE username = ? AND password_hash = ?", (username, password_hash))
    result = c.fetchone()
    conn.close()
    if result:
